# Interface Contracts: Claude-Audit Quality

**Purpose**: Single source of truth for every NEW frontmatter shape, file path schema, output-rendering contract, and rubric grammar extension this PR introduces. Implementation in `plugin-kiln/skills/kiln-claude-audit/SKILL.md`, `plugin-kiln/skills/kiln-doctor/SKILL.md`, `plugin-kiln/rubrics/claude-md-usefulness.md`, `plugin-kiln/rubrics/retro-quality.md`, and the retrospective agent prompt MUST match these contracts exactly.

**Generated by**: specifier (`/plan` phase) for `build/claude-audit-quality-20260425`
**Consumed by**: `impl-claude-audit` (§§1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6) and `impl-tests-and-retro` (§§7, 8 + fixture stubs in §9)

This file pins seven contract surfaces:
1. Rubric rule schema (extended with `signal_type: substance` and optional `ctx_json_paths:` field).
2. The four substance rules + `recent-changes-anti-pattern` rule (full frontmatter text).
3. Editorial-pass discipline / `inconclusive` trigger taxonomy (rubric preamble change).
4. Audit output ordering (Signal Summary + Notes section sort key).
5. Sibling preview file path schema + post-apply state shape.
6. Audit log cross-reference text + footer cleanup string.
7. Retro frontmatter keys + retro substance rubric file shape.

Plus §9: fixture path conventions (informational; consumed by `impl-tests-and-retro`).

---

## §1 — Rubric rule schema (extended)

The existing rubric uses a per-rule fenced YAML-ish block (see prior `claude-md-audit-reframe` PR for shape):

```yaml
rule_id: <stable-id>
signal_type: <load-bearing | editorial | freshness | bloat | coverage | substance>
cost: <cheap | editorial>
match_rule: <one-line description; for substance rules MUST name CTX_JSON path(s)>
action: <keep | archive-candidate | removal-candidate | duplication-flag | expand-candidate | sync-candidate | correction-candidate>
ctx_json_paths: [<path1>, <path2>, ...]   # OPTIONAL; required when match_rule reads CTX_JSON; substance rules MUST set this
rationale: <one-sentence rationale>
cached: <bool>
```

### Schema changes this PR introduces

- **`signal_type`** enum gains the value `substance`. Existing values (`load-bearing`, `editorial`, `freshness`, `bloat`, `coverage`) are preserved per NFR-004. The value `external` is NOT added — external best-practices deltas remain in their own `## External best-practices deltas` section per NFR-004 reconciliation.
- **`ctx_json_paths`** field is new and optional. When the `match_rule` reads from `CTX_JSON` (the deterministic snapshot emitted by `plugin-kiln/scripts/context/read-project-context.sh`), the rule MUST list every path it reads. Substance rules MUST populate this field (it is the FR-013 enforcement anchor — the audit emits `(no project-context signals fired)` only when zero rules with non-empty `ctx_json_paths:` fire).

### `CTX_JSON` paths recognized by this PR

Per `plugin-kiln/scripts/context/read-project-context.sh` schema (assumed stable; not modified in this PR):

| Path | Source | Used by rule |
|---|---|---|
| `vision.body` | `.kiln/vision.md` body text | `missing-thesis` (FR-006), `missing-loop` (FR-007), `scaffold-undertaught` (FR-009) |
| `roadmap.items` | `.kiln/roadmap/items/*.md` (frontmatter + body excerpts) | `missing-loop` (FR-007 — phase-status check) |
| `roadmap.phases` | `.kiln/roadmap/phases/*.md` (status + name) | `missing-loop` (FR-007) |
| `plugins.list` | enabled plugin enumeration (project + user `enabledPlugins`) | `missing-architectural-context` (FR-008) |
| `claude_md.body` | `CLAUDE.md` body | `scaffold-undertaught` (FR-009 — source-vs-scaffold concept comparison) |
| `readme.body` | `README.md` body (when present) | (not used by rules in this PR; field reserved) |

---

## §2 — New rules added by this feature

Each rule below MUST appear in `plugin-kiln/rubrics/claude-md-usefulness.md` as a `### <rule_id>` heading followed by the fenced block, then prose (rationale + known false-positive shape). Order within the rubric file: substance rules grouped under a `## Substance rules` heading, listed in FR order (006, 007, 008, 009); `recent-changes-anti-pattern` lives under whatever existing section groups the freshness/bloat rules (impl picks the location to minimize churn).

### `missing-thesis` (FR-006)

```yaml
rule_id: missing-thesis
signal_type: substance
cost: editorial
match_rule: read CTX_JSON.vision.body; extract vision-pillar phrases (vision.md ^## headings + first paragraph); pre-filter the audited file's opener + ## What This Repo Is body via grep for any pillar phrase; if pre-filter returns 0 hits, invoke editorial pass to confirm absence
action: expand-candidate
ctx_json_paths: [vision.body]
rationale: A CLAUDE.md that doesn't name the project's thesis lets Claude operate without anchoring product intent to mechanics. Vision pillars are the highest-leverage content.
cached: false
```

### `missing-loop` (FR-007)

```yaml
rule_id: missing-loop
signal_type: substance
cost: editorial
match_rule: read CTX_JSON.vision.body + CTX_JSON.roadmap.phases; if any phase has status: in-progress or status: complete (i.e., the project has shipped or is shipping a feedback loop), AND the audited file does not name the loop's input → consumer → output triple, fire
action: expand-candidate
ctx_json_paths: [vision.body, roadmap.phases]
rationale: A capture surface (issues, feedback, roadmap, mistakes, fixes) without a named consumer becomes an isolated tool; the loop is the product per .kiln/vision.md.
cached: false
```

### `missing-architectural-context` (FR-008)

```yaml
rule_id: missing-architectural-context
signal_type: substance
cost: cheap
match_rule: count distinct plugin-*/ roots from CTX_JSON.plugins.list; if count > 1, parse the audited file's ## Architecture section (or equivalent ## Architecture-tagged heading); fire if section describes only one plugin or is absent
action: expand-candidate
ctx_json_paths: [plugins.list]
rationale: Multi-plugin repos have architectural surface that one-plugin Architecture sections silently hide. Documenting only one plugin teaches the wrong mental model.
cached: false
```

### `scaffold-undertaught` (FR-009)

```yaml
rule_id: scaffold-undertaught
signal_type: substance
cost: editorial
match_rule: applies only when audited file path matches plugin-*/scaffold/CLAUDE.md (or repo's documented scaffold-template glob); read CTX_JSON.claude_md.body (source-repo CLAUDE.md); for each load-bearing concept family in the source — (a) thesis (vision pillar), (b) loop (input → consumer → output), (c) architectural pointer (e.g. "scaffold deploys into consumer projects via X") — verify the scaffold communicates the same concept; fire per missing concept family
action: expand-candidate
ctx_json_paths: [claude_md.body, vision.body]
rationale: Scaffolds are seeds; they propagate the load-bearing concepts the source repo teaches. A scaffold that omits thesis / loop / architecture seeds Claude with a mechanics-only template.
cached: false
```

### `recent-changes-anti-pattern` (FR-016)

```yaml
rule_id: recent-changes-anti-pattern
signal_type: substance
cost: cheap
match_rule: presence of literal "## Recent Changes" heading in the audited file
action: removal-candidate
ctx_json_paths: []
rationale: A ## Recent Changes section becomes a churn surface and circular-load-bearing protection (rules cite it because it exists; it exists because rules cite it). git log + roadmap phases + ls docs/features/ + /kiln:kiln-next collectively cover the same need without churn.
cached: false
```

The proposed-diff body for `recent-changes-anti-pattern` MUST replace the entire `## Recent Changes` section with this exact text (OQ-4 reconciliation: generic placeholder for byte-identity across re-runs):

```markdown
## Looking up recent changes

This file does not maintain a running changelog. To find recent changes:
- `git log --oneline -n 20` — commit-level history.
- `.kiln/roadmap/phases/<active-phase>.md` — phase-level status (in-progress, complete, planned items).
- `ls docs/features/` — shipped feature PRDs.
- `/kiln:kiln-next` — current session-pickup recommendations.
```

The audit log's Notes section MAY include a one-line companion comment naming the current phase (e.g. `current phase: 10-self-optimization`); this is in Notes, NOT in the diff body, so byte-identity is preserved.

---

## §3 — Editorial-pass discipline + `inconclusive` trigger taxonomy (FR-003 / FR-004)

The rubric preamble (top of `plugin-kiln/rubrics/claude-md-usefulness.md`) MUST contain the following sub-section verbatim (or with adjusted heading level):

```markdown
### When `inconclusive` is legitimate

The model running `/kiln:kiln-claude-audit` performs editorial evaluation in its own context — there is no sub-LLM call. For each editorial rule, the skill MUST load the reference document(s), read every `^## ` section, compare per `match_rule`, and emit findings or `(no fire)`. Skipping the comparison and marking `inconclusive` is forbidden.

The audit MAY emit `inconclusive` ONLY for these three triggers:

1. **Missing reference document** — the rule's `match_rule:` requires a file (e.g. `.specify/memory/constitution.md`, `.kiln/vision.md`, source-repo `CLAUDE.md`) that is not present on disk.
2. **Unparseable reference** — the file exists but is malformed in a way that breaks the `match_rule:` (e.g. invalid YAML frontmatter, encoding error, truncated).
3. **External dependency unavailable** — the rule depends on a remote fetch (WebFetch / MCP) that returned non-2xx or timed out.

"Editorial work feels expensive" is explicitly NOT a legitimate trigger. The Notes cell of an `inconclusive` row MUST cite the specific trigger and the specific resource (e.g. `inconclusive — reference document .specify/memory/constitution.md not found on disk`).
```

Cross-reference: FR-031 of `claude-md-audit-reframe` (where `enumeration-bloat` wins over `load-bearing-section` for `plugin-surface` sections) is reinforced by FR-018's load-bearing reword — add a one-line note in the preamble: "FR-018 load-bearing rewording aligns with FR-031 of `claude-md-audit-reframe`: a section is load-bearing only when cited from skill/agent/hook/workflow PROSE, not from a rule's `match_rule:` field."

---

## §4 — Audit output ordering (FR-010 / FR-015)

### `## Signal Summary` table sort key

The Signal Summary's row sort key is `(signal_type_rank, severity_rank, rule_id)` ASCENDING:

| `signal_type` | `signal_type_rank` |
|---|---|
| `substance` | 0 |
| `coverage` | 1 |
| `editorial` | 2 |
| `freshness` | 3 |
| `bloat` | 4 |
| `load-bearing` | 5 |

Rationale: substance findings lead by FR-010. Within a rank, secondary sort is the existing severity_rank → rule_id tie-break.

### Skill body section ordering (FR-015)

`kiln-claude-audit/SKILL.md` step ordering is updated so the substance pass runs before the cheap rubric pass:

```
Step 1   — read project context (CTX_JSON)
Step 2   — substance pass (FR-006..FR-009 + FR-016)        ← was Step 3 cheap rubric pass; substance now first
Step 3   — cheap rubric pass (existing freshness / bloat / load-bearing rules)
Step 3.5 — output discipline invariant check (FR-001) — every fired signal has an artifact
Step 4   — render audit log
Step 4.5 — render sibling preview files (FR-021)
Step 5   — emit external best-practices delta sub-section
Step 6   — write audit log + sibling previews to .kiln/logs/
```

The audit log's section ordering matches: `## Signal Summary` (sorted as above) → `## Notes` (substance findings before mechanical) → `## Proposed Diff` → `## External best-practices deltas`.

### Project-context-driven row guarantee (FR-013)

After Step 3, the skill MUST inspect the fired-signals list. If zero rules with non-empty `ctx_json_paths:` fired, the skill MUST emit a literal placeholder row in the Signal Summary:

```
| (no project-context signals fired) | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |
```

(Column count matches the existing Signal Summary table shape; cells are em-dashes except the rule_id cell.)

---

## §5 — Sibling preview file path schema (FR-020 / FR-021)

### Path schema

```
.kiln/logs/claude-md-audit-<TIMESTAMP>-proposed-<basename>.md
```

Where:
- `<TIMESTAMP>` is the same UTC timestamp used in the main audit log filename (e.g. `2026-04-25-202320`). One sibling preview per audited path with ≥1 proposed diff shares the timestamp with the audit log.
- `<basename>` is derived from the audited file's repo-relative path by replacing every `/` with `-`. Examples:
  - `CLAUDE.md` → `CLAUDE.md`
  - `plugin-kiln/scaffold/CLAUDE.md` → `plugin-kiln-scaffold-CLAUDE.md`

Resulting full filenames for the kiln source repo (which audits both files):
- `.kiln/logs/claude-md-audit-2026-04-25-202320-proposed-CLAUDE.md`
- `.kiln/logs/claude-md-audit-2026-04-25-202320-proposed-plugin-kiln-scaffold-CLAUDE.md`

### Post-apply state shape

The sibling preview file contains the proposed final state of the audited file — i.e., the exact bytes the audited file would have if every proposed diff in the main audit log were `git apply`ed. The preview is written verbatim (no diff markers, no annotations); it IS the post-apply file.

### Rendering rule

A sibling preview file is rendered ONLY when the audited path has ≥1 proposed diff in the main audit log. If all signals for an audited path are `keep` / `inconclusive`, no sibling preview is rendered for that path.

### Permitted-files list update

`kiln-claude-audit/SKILL.md` permitted-files list (the explicit allowlist of paths the skill writes) gains:

```
.kiln/logs/claude-md-audit-<TIMESTAMP>-proposed-<basename>.md
```

with `<TIMESTAMP>` and `<basename>` matching the schema above.

---

## §6 — Audit log cross-reference text + footer cleanup string

### Cross-reference (FR-022)

The audit log's `## Proposed Diff` heading is followed by exactly one cross-reference line per audited path that has a sibling preview:

```markdown
## Proposed Diff

Side-by-side preview: see `claude-md-audit-<TIMESTAMP>-proposed-<basename>.md`.
```

If multiple audited paths each have a sibling preview, render one such line per path (in audited-path order).

### Footer cleanup string (FR-023)

The audit log's footer (last line before EOF) is exactly:

```markdown
Once proposed diffs land, this audit log + sibling preview files can be archived to `.kiln/logs/archive/` or deleted.
```

This is a static string. NO timestamp interpolation; NO conditional rendering — the line is always present.

---

## §7 — Retro frontmatter keys (FR-024)

The `kiln-build-prd` retrospective agent emits its retrospective as a GitHub issue. The issue body's YAML frontmatter MUST contain these two keys (in addition to any existing keys):

```yaml
insight_score: <integer 1-5>
insight_score_justification: <one-line string; max ~120 chars; cites the rubric criterion that drove the score>
```

### Self-rating prompt (FR-024 + FR-025)

The retrospective agent's prompt template gains a self-rating block that:

1. Reads `plugin-kiln/rubrics/retro-quality.md` verbatim.
2. Applies the rubric's three-criterion test (cause-and-effect / calibration update / process change) to the retro body the agent just drafted.
3. Emits `insight_score:` 1-5 (1 = none of the criteria met; 5 = all three met with non-obvious content).
4. Emits a one-line `insight_score_justification:` naming which criterion drove the score.

### Team-lead surfacing (FR-024)

The team-lead's pipeline-summary message (final wrap-up of `/kiln:kiln-build-prd`) MUST inspect the retro issue's frontmatter. If `insight_score < 3` (default threshold), the summary MUST include this line verbatim (with substitution):

```
⚠ Low-substance retrospective — insight_score: <N>. Justification: <justification>.
```

If `insight_score >= 3`, no warning is emitted (the score is logged in the issue body for posterity but doesn't surface in summary).

The threshold `3` is the default; future PRs MAY make it configurable via a `kiln-build-prd` step option, but in this PR it is hardcoded.

---

## §8 — Retro substance rubric file shape (FR-025)

`plugin-kiln/rubrics/retro-quality.md` is a NEW file with this shape:

```markdown
# Retrospective Quality Rubric

**Purpose**: Define the minimum substance bar for `/kiln:kiln-build-prd` retrospective issues. Used by the retrospective agent's self-rating prompt (FR-024 of `claude-audit-quality`).

## What counts as a high-substance retro

A high-substance retro contains at least ONE of the following:

1. **Non-obvious cause-and-effect claim** — names a specific failure mode in this run AND attributes it to a specific root cause that wasn't already documented in the spec / plan. "Tests failed because the fixture path was wrong" is obvious; "tests failed because the agent assumed `WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_DIR` was env-inherited but harness-spawned sub-agents don't inherit env" is non-obvious and process-relevant.
2. **Calibration update with reasoning** — observes that a prior estimate (timing, complexity, blast radius, review cost) was wrong, AND offers a one-sentence reason the model now believes a different estimate would be correct next time.
3. **Process-change proposal** — proposes a concrete change to a skill / agent / hook / workflow / template (the "PI proposals" pattern), with the file named, the current behavior described, and the proposed behavior described. Bold-inline format preferred.

## Rating scale

| Score | Meaning |
|---|---|
| 1 | None of the three criteria met. The retro is a status report. |
| 2 | One criterion partially met (e.g. names a failure but no root cause). |
| 3 | One criterion fully met. (Default threshold for "passes" — below this fires the team-lead warning.) |
| 4 | Two criteria fully met. |
| 5 | All three criteria fully met, with non-obvious content. |

## How the agent self-rates

The retrospective agent applies this rubric verbatim to the retro body it just drafted. It MUST emit:

```yaml
insight_score: <integer 1-5>
insight_score_justification: <one-line; cites the criterion(a) driving the score>
```

The justification names the criterion(a) that drove the score — e.g. "process-change proposal: bold-inline PI for require-feature-branch.sh". Honest self-rating is the contract; if the retro is a status report, the agent emits `1`, not `3` to game the threshold.
```

---

## §9 — Fixture path conventions (informational)

Five fixture directories under `plugin-kiln/tests/`. Each is self-contained per NFR-002 (no network calls; runs via `/kiln:kiln-test plugin-kiln <fixture>`):

| Path | Anchors | Asserts |
|---|---|---|
| `plugin-kiln/tests/claude-audit-no-comment-only-hunks/` | FR-002 / SC-001 | Audit output contains zero `# ... No diff proposed` lines when `external/length-density` rule fires |
| `plugin-kiln/tests/claude-audit-editorial-pass-required/` | FR-005 / SC-002 | `duplicated-in-constitution` fires with `action: duplication-flag` (NOT `inconclusive`) on a CLAUDE.md paraphrasing constitution content |
| `plugin-kiln/tests/claude-audit-substance/` | FR-011 / SC-003 | `missing-thesis` fires on a structurally-clean CLAUDE.md with no vision-pillar reference |
| `plugin-kiln/tests/claude-audit-grounded-finding-required/` | FR-014 / SC-004 | At least one substance finding fires with primary-justification citation of `CTX_JSON` content; rationale line is present + non-empty |
| `plugin-kiln/tests/claude-audit-recent-changes-anti-pattern/` | FR-019 / SC-005 | `recent-changes-anti-pattern` fires with a removal-candidate diff against a CLAUDE.md containing `## Recent Changes` |

Each fixture follows the existing kiln-test convention (`run.sh` + scaffolded `.kiln/` per fixture's needs + assertions emitted to stdout in the harness's expected format). `impl-tests-and-retro` owns authoring; the contracts above are sufficient for the implementer to write fixtures without further specifier guidance.
